subject: Add xone (XBox wireless adapter) driver.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: Xbox One wireless adapters, Xbox Series controllers, wireless chatpad, and wireless headsets would not be supported unless provided via separate out-of-tree kmod package

notes: This commit vendors the xone driver (7 kernel modules: xone-wired, xone-dongle, xone-gip-bus, xone-gip-common, xone-gip-gamepad, xone-gip-headset, xone-gip-chatpad) from medusalix/xone (GPL-2.0-or-later). Verified: JOYSTICK_XONE does not exist in vanilla 6.18.38. Per docs/patch-provenance.md §3.5, this commit was later re-sourced as a Buildroot kmod package (package/xone) using dlundqvist/xone (the actively-maintained fork; medusalix/xone is explicitly in maintenance mode per its own README). The Buildroot package approach is superior for 6.18 reconciliation: (1) avoids vendoring out-of-tree code in the kernel; (2) enables independent driver updates without full kernel rebuilds; (3) upstream fork (dlundqvist) already includes all MiSTer's local enhancements (rumble support, Elite-2 paddle support with PADDLE_ELITE/PADDLE_ELITE2_4X/PADDLE_ELITE2_510 firmware-version cases, per-PID firmware naming, sysfs software pairing attribute). Failure mode is silent: dropping this commit and relying solely on the Buildroot package (enabled via BR2_PACKAGE_XONE=y) preserves all functionality. No userspace coupling found in Main_MiSTer. Disposition: dropped-deliberate in favor of re-sourcing via package/xone. sonnet-verified: correct — vendored commit's own SPDX/copyright headers (GPL-2.0-or-later, Severin von Wnuck, medusalix/xone) confirmed against the fork's git history at 4ddd8ec3d; the 7-module Makefile list (xone-wired/xone-dongle/xone-gip-bus/xone-gip-common/xone-gip-gamepad/xone-gip-headset/xone-gip-chatpad) confirmed exactly; JOYSTICK_XONE absent from vanilla 6.18.38 confirmed. Device_ids lists only the two externally-attachable dongle PIDs (02e6, 02fe) of the four the driver's table actually matches (02f9/091e are laptop-soldered dongles that cannot physically attach to a DE10-Nano, consistent with docs/decisions/0003-xone-firmware.md's scoping) — narrower than the full ID table but not incorrect for MiSTer's actual hardware universe, left as-is.
